title: Parallelize batch work with xargs -P safely
description: Fan out independent work across cores with xargs -P or GNU parallel using NUL-delimited input, bounded concurrency, and per-item failure handling.
body: Use when processing many independent files or items and the work is IO/CPU parallelizable. Do not pipe space/newline-split filenames (they break on spaces) or launch unbounded background jobs that thrash the machine. Actions: feed NUL-delimited input (`find . -print0 | xargs -0 -P "$(nproc)" -n1 cmd`), bound -P to core count, make each item's command self-contained, and capture per-item exit status rather than assuming success. Validation signals include processed-count matching input-count and a non-zero overall status when any item fails. Risk boundary: parallel writers to one output file interleave and corrupt it; write to per-item temp outputs and merge, and escalate if items share mutable state instead of forcing concurrency.
